decisions.briefing: Markets sold off sharply today with NASDAQ down 2.3% and S&P 500 down 1.3%, putting conditions squarely in risk-off territory. No screened candidates were available and existing positions are being held per strategy — stop-loss and profit-target rules will handle any position adjustments automatically rather than reacting to one bad day.

market_education.summary_en: Broad-based selling hit growth and tech-heavy names hardest today, dragging the NASDAQ down more than the S&P 500, a pattern typical of risk-off days where investors rotate out of higher-beta stocks. Without specific confirmed headlines available today, the move appears consistent with general risk-aversion rather than a single company-specific catalyst. Staying patient and avoiding forced trades is the appropriate response to this kind of broad, sentiment-driven decline.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場出現全面拋售,科技股及成長股受創最深,拖累納指跌幅超過標普500指數,這是典型的「risk-off」(避險)交易日特徵,投資者從高波動性股票撤資。由於今日未有具體確認的新聞頭條可供引用,此次下跌看來屬於整體市場避險情緒所致,而非個別公司的特定因素。在這種廣泛且由市場情緒主導的下跌中,保持耐性、避免強行交易是較為恰當的應對方式。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors broadly sell riskier assets like growth stocks and move toward safer holdings, often triggered by economic worry or uncertainty. On days like this, it's usually smarter to hold your existing positions and let automatic stop-loss and profit-target rules manage risk, rather than making new bets into the downturn.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「Risk-Off」(避險)交易日是指投資者普遍拋售成長股等高風險資產,轉向較安全的資產,通常由經濟憂慮或不確定性所觸發。在這類日子,通常較明智的做法是持有現有倉位,讓自動止損和獲利目標機制發揮作用管理風險,而不是在市場下跌時進行新的投資。
